name: Legionaries
note: Professional Roman heavy infantry; the numerical profile is a bounded technical role adapter

name: Auxilia
note: Roman levy-analog with an explicitly non-mechanical citizenship-on-discharge flavor

name: Roman Alae
note: Bounded early-imperial auxiliary-cavalry role adapter; it does not reconstruct an ala name strength or provincial posting

name: Thureophoroi
note: Hellenistic flexible-infantry adapter; regional availability is deliberately bounded

name: Hellenistic Phalanx
note: Remnant phalanx adapter for the Black Sea Hellenistic courts rather than a universal classical unit

name: Warbands
note: Germanic Celtic and Brittonic levy-warband adapter; it does not claim a uniform tactical doctrine

name: Han Crossbow Infantry
note: Western Han regular crossbow-infantry edge represented without inventing a specific unit establishment

name: Indian Longbowmen
note: Regional longbow infantry adapter for the Indian subcontinent and Lanka

name: War Elephants
note: War-elephant adapter restricted to South Asian courts; its exact local supply is later economy work

name: Nubian Archers
note: Nile-valley and Horn archer adapter without claiming a single uniform ethnic establishment

name: Cataphracts
note: Iranian and Sarmatian heavy-lancer adapter; Roman adoption remains a later advance-era outcome

name: Parthian Horse Archers
note: Iranian mounted-archery role adapter complementary to cataphracts; it does not fix a noble-house contingent or field-army composition

name: Steppe Horse Archers
note: Mounted-archery adapter for Xiongnu and adjacent steppe polities

name: Numidian Light Horse
note: North African light-cavalry adapter without projecting Roman auxiliary organization onto independent courts

name: Camelry
note: Arabian camel-mounted adapter with intentionally broad regional availability

name: British Chariots
note: Obsolescent Brittonic chariot adapter rather than an assertion that every AD 1 community fielded the same force

name: Germanic Light Horse
note: Bounded Germanic and Celtic light-horse levy adapter; it does not claim a uniform tribal cavalry doctrine equipment ratio or campaign formation

name: Comitatenses
note: Late Roman mobile-field-army adapter reserved for the Dominate age

name: Limitanei
note: Late Roman frontier-force adapter reserved for the Dominate age

name: Balearic Slingers
note: Era mercenary company adapter; availability is not a claim of permanent island sovereignty

name: Cretan Archers
note: Era mercenary company adapter for Hellenic military labor markets

name: Germanic Bodyguards
note: Era mercenary-company adapter; it does not pre-script a named guard corps

name: Saka Horse
note: Era mercenary-company adapter for Saka-style mounted archers

name: Galatian Swordsmen
note: Era mercenary-company adapter for Hellenistic and Roman military labor markets; it does not create a named Galatian regiment

name: Thracian Peltasts
note: Era light-infantry company adapter for Balkan and Hellenistic military labor markets without a fixed ethnic order of battle

name: Numidian Horse Company
note: Era North African light-horse company adapter distinct from independent-court forces and without a named contract or roster

name: Liburnian
note: Light Roman galley with explicitly zero gunpowder armament

name: Trireme
note: Oared Mediterranean warship adapter with explicitly zero gunpowder armament

name: Quinquereme
note: Larger Hellenistic-Roman galley adapter with explicitly zero gunpowder armament

name: Merchant Roundship
note: Ancient merchant carrier adapter; it is not an ocean-crossing early-modern class

name: Monsoon Dhow
note: Indian-Ocean coastal and monsoon vessel adapter with explicitly zero gunpowder armament

name: Austronesian Outrigger
note: Austronesian small-vessel adapter; no trans-Pacific discovery or oceanic class is implied
